name: Red Cabbage Salad (Prodigy)
recipeIngredient: 20 Walnuts; shelled and broken into quarters or eighths
3 tbsp Light or virgin olive oil
1 small Red onion; quartered and thinly sliced crosswise
4 oz Soft goat cheese; crumbled =OR= Feta Cheese, crumbled
recipeInstructions:
1. Toss the shelled walnuts in a little walnut oil to coat lightly, season with salt and pepper, and spread on a baking sheet. Toast at 350°F about 7 minutes until they begin to have a good, toasty smell.
2. Cut cabbage into quarters or sixths so that pieces are no more than about 2-1/2 inches across. Remove cores and slice pieces very thinly. Set aside.
3. Cut apple into sixths, remove cores, then thinly slice pieces crosswise.
4. In a wide sauté pan over medium-high heat, combine vinegar and olive oil. When hot, add onion and sauté 30 seconds.
5. Add cabbage to the pan and stir with tongs about 2 minutes or until just wilted. Leaves will begin to soften and color will change from bright purple-red to pink.
6. Season to taste with salt, plenty of pepper, and more vinegar if necessary to sharpen flavors.
7. Add most of the cheese and parsley, all the apples and walnuts. Toss briefly to combine, garnish with remaining cheese and parsley, and serve.
